description: By participating in the retail laboratory, learn what it takes to become a retail entrepreneur and the small-business startup industry by getting hands-on, real-world experience in marketing, human resource management, merchandise planning, public relations, event management, visual merchandising, e-commerce, and the fundamentals of business operations. Understand entrepreneurship and the skills required to be successful by working with entrepreneurial mentors and skilled industry experts focused on the latest industry trends.This course is targeted towards sophomores, juniors, and seniors who are interested in starting their own retail business or who want to further their entrepreneurial skill set to empower them in their future careers. This class is focused on the retail industry but is open to all majors, including apparel merchandising and design, event management, graphic design, finance, marketing, and more!Students who interviewed for and were awarded the director positions for running Innovate 1858 enroll at the 4000 level and managerial level at the 3000 level, while all others chose to participate in departments enrolled at the 2000 level.

Required of all new AESHM graduate students. Offered on a satisfactory-fail basis only.

prerequisites: Graduate classification

prerequisites: STAT 5101 or equivalent; Graduate standing in the Department; Permission of Instructor
description: Overview of quantitative research methods in apparel and hospitality fields. Topics include types of quantitative research design, sampling design, measurement, validity issues, power and precision analysis, methods of data gathering and analysis techniques, and interpretation of statistical results. Use of statistical packages. Development of research plan. Offered odd-numbered years.
